insight:
Yesterday looked solid overall! Your 12,680 steps show you stayed active despite no formal training sessions, and burning 2,513 calories suggests your metabolism is firing well. Your resting heart rate of 50 bpm demonstrates excellent cardiovascular fitness, and that low stress level of 31 shows your body is managing the training load beautifully as we approach race day.

Your HRV of 106ms sits perfectly within your baseline range of 90-112ms, indicating your body recovered well and is ready for today's planned session. Combined with that low resting heart rate, you're primed to tackle whatever training is on the schedule without needing to dial back intensity or volume.

However, that 6.3 hours of sleep is cutting it close for optimal recovery. Tonight, prioritize getting to bed 30 minutes earlier to hit closer to 7-8 hours. Your body is clearly adapting well to training, but more sleep will supercharge your recovery as we fine-tune for Chester in just 15 days.
